No Poverty.reason: No explicit or implicit evidence in the course syllabus supports this SDG.

Zero Hunger.reason: No explicit or implicit evidence in the course syllabus supports this SDG.

Good Health and Well-being.reason: The course explicitly covers 'Sports & Health' (Weeks 14, 15), including 'Alzheimer’s disease' and 'How to Stay Well,' directly aligning with Target 3.4.

Quality Education.reason: As 'College English II,' it's tertiary education (Target 4.3). The course aims to develop 'Global Citizens' and 'critical engagement,' directly supporting global citizenship education (Target 4.7).
evidence: be prepared for learning in a higher education environment

Gender Equality.reason: The course discusses 'Malala Yousafzai' (Week 2), a symbol for female education, and 'PC Language' (Weeks 10, 11), promoting non-discrimination, aligning with Targets 5.1 and 5.5.

Clean Water and Sanitation.reason: Topics like 'Pristine Places,' 'Deep Blue Sea,' and 'Exploring the Okavango Delta' (Week 4) relate to protecting water-related ecosystems, aligning with Target 6.6.

Affordable and Clean Energy.reason: No explicit or implicit evidence in the course syllabus supports this SDG.

Decent Work and Economic Growth.reason: The 'Further Study & Job Prospects' section (Weeks 12, 13), including 'Cover letter + CV' and 'Interview tips,' directly supports preparing for decent work (Target 8.5).

Industry, Innovation and Infrastructure.reason: The core competence 'Speculation and Innovation Capacity' provides a tenuous, inferred link to encouraging innovation (Target 9.5).
evidence: 思辨與創新能力 Speculation and Innovation Capacity

Reduced Inequalities.reason: Discussions on 'PC Language' (Weeks 10, 11) and 'cultural differences' (learning outcome) promote inclusive practices and reduce discrimination, aligning with Targets 10.2 and 10.3.

Sustainable Cities and Communities.reason: The topic 'World Heritages Sites' (Week 5) directly relates to protecting the world's cultural and natural heritage, aligning with Target 11.4.

Responsible Consumption and Production.reason: Discussions on 'Environmental issues' and 'ecological issues' (Weeks 4, 6) raise awareness for sustainable lifestyles, an inferred link to Target 12.8.

Climate Action.reason: The course covers 'Environmental issues' and 'ecological issues' (Weeks 4, 6), contributing to education and awareness-raising on climate change impacts, aligning with Target 13.3.

Life Below Water.reason: Explicit discussions on the 'Deep Blue Sea' (Week 4) and 'An Underwater Museum' (TED TALK, Week 5) directly raise awareness about protecting marine ecosystems (Target 14.2).
evidence: An Underwater Museum, Teeming with Life

Life on Land.reason: Topics like 'Pristine Places,' 'Exploring the Okavango Delta' (Week 4), and 'ecological issues' (Weeks 4, 6) relate to conserving terrestrial ecosystems, aligning with Target 15.1.

Peace, Justice and Strong Institutions.reason: Discussions on 'Malala Yousafzai' (Week 2), 'Global Citizens' (Weeks 2, 3), and 'PC Language' (Weeks 10, 11) touch on themes of peace, justice, and non-discrimination (Target 16.b).

Partnerships for the Goals.reason: The course develops 'International Mobility' and 'cross-cultural communication' skills, foundational for building global partnerships and cooperation (Targets 17.6, 17.16).
